title: How to start containers with this module?
body: Hi I have this python module installed and a running socket, I am looking for a way to start a container with this module, similar to the docker.Client(version="1.22").start(some_container). I could find not find the relevant command with this podman module. Any help would be really great :)

comments.body: The code is still pending in #46
Feel free to pull down that version and let me know if it doesn't work in that PR.
Okay, will try with that pull :)

Is it also possible to add functions like `exec _start` which starts the container with the given command?
Also in `containers.create` command instead of full `data` fields can it be modularised with extra params like `image`

That doesn't look available via the API. https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodStartContainer  If you know of a way to do that via the api we should just modify the start container. But that doesn't appear to be possible per the docs.

As for the create command, we don't want to be managing parameter mappings across versions in this module. The goal of this is to be a minimal layer for interaction with the API.  The problem with us having to compose the data, would be that this python module would need to be more version aware and constantly updated if the parameters change. I think it's better for you to do that create data mapping in your application if you only need to customize a few things rather than putting it in this module itself.

See https://pypi.org/project/podman-py/

```
with podman.PodmanClient() as client:
    image = client.images.pull("quay.io/libpod/alpine", tag="latest")
    container = client.containers.create(image, command=["echo", random_string])
    container.start()
    ....

```
